_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identifying the underlying principles, reasons, or facts of information by breaking down information or data into separate parts.
reference_document_element_description: Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
comments: Near-exact match. Data analysis is WIDAI's core domain. The entire AB domain, plus AI analytical tasks, DQ profiling, and RM risk analysis. O*NET describes the general activity; WIDAI defines it in extensive depth across multiple specializations.
_comment_atlas_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score for an Equal relationship reflects vocabulary divergence between O*NET's general-purpose language and WIDAI's domain-specific terminology. The concepts are equivalent despite surface-level phrasing differences that compress the cross-encoder score.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8507 indicates lower token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. Balanced P/R is consistent with the Equal classification — both descriptions cover each other's semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (97.82%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral for an Equal pair reflects vocabulary divergence rather than conceptual divergence — the NLI model processes surface language, and cross-domain terminology differences suppress the entailment signal even when the underlying concepts align.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.4411) is close to the STS cross-encoder (0.4711). The small gap (0.03) suggests the semantic relationship is largely capturable through independent encoding — the descriptions share enough surface-level semantic features that cross-attention adds only marginal signal.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (6.25% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
